taxonomy: Struthanthus vulgaris is classified as a species of mistletoe in the family Loranthaceae. This family is a group of parasitic plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Loranthaceae is further divided into several genera, with Struthanthus being one of them. Struthanthus vulgaris is a type of hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients from the host plant while also photosynthesizing. The classification of Struthanthus vulgaris is a part of the broader understanding of the plant's unique adaptations and survival strategies.
